scope: Pass B full-surface expansion, re-reviewed against the live developer docs (docs.countercyclical.io/developers/*, fetched 2026-07-04, which document a materially larger surface than the upstream Airbyte-source-shaped legacy connector's 3-stream manifest): 6 read streams (the 3 legacy streams retained, plus teams/assumptions/pipelines) and 1 write action (create_investment). Every documented endpoint is covered_by a stream/write action XOR excluded with a real category and reason. Webhooks are documented as a settings-UI-managed Enterprise feature with no CRUD API endpoint at all (non_data_endpoint); the two integration-specific investment-creation actions (Zapier and Make) are functionally identical (same apiKey query-param auth every other endpoint uses, same tickerSymbol-only request body) so only one is exposed as a write action to avoid declaring two write actions with byte-identical behavior.

excluded.reason: Make-app credential-validation probe, not a syncable object or a record mutation
Make-scenario-trigger polling endpoint for newly created investments; identical record shape to the already-covered investments list stream

functionally identical to create_investment (POST .../make/actions/investments): same apiKey auth, same tickerSymbol-only request body, same investment-creation effect; declaring both would be two write actions with byte-identical behavior differing only in URL path
Zapier-app credential-validation probe, not a syncable object or a record mutation
Zapier-trigger polling endpoint for newly created investments; identical record shape to the already-covered investments list stream
webhook management is documented as a Settings-UI-only Enterprise feature (Settings > Workspace > Advanced > Developers); no CRUD API endpoint is published for creating/listing/deleting webhooks
